p.strong: Inlining erases function boundaries at the machine level.

ul.li: The compiler physically copies the code
No call instruction = no stack frame
Symbol table has no entry for inlined function
Even with -g, the address range is gone DWARF .debug_info can map instruction ranges back to original function names, but this requires:

ol.li: Profiler support for DWARF inline expansion
Binary not stripped of debug sections

g.text: Flame Graph (what you WANT to see)
Shows validation and transformation as separate hotspots
All time attributed to process() - hotspots invisible!
DWARF preserves the mapping: address range → original function name
